item.title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.

item.title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.

item.title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

item.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech—a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

item.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that let's it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

item.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Otter is also releasing a new Windows app that can capture meeting notes without joining one

item.title: Drizzle on top: a new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1%
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

item.title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

item.title: Letterboxd, the social platform for film buffs, reportedly looking for new owner
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Potential buyers of Letterboxd include Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, and Hollywood media company The Ankler, according to Semafor.

item.title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Hacker who allegedly carried out cyberattacks for China is extradited to US
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Xu Zewei is accused of participating in a Chinese government hacking group that broke into thousands of U.S. organizations and stole COVID-19-related research.

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

item.title: Data center demand drives 66% surge in natural gas power plant costs
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Natural gas power plant costs have nearly doubled in two years and take 23% longer to build as data center electricity demand skyrockets.

item.title: What’s behind Europe’s efforts to ditch US software in favor of sovereign tech
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Governments across Europe are looking to rely less on American tech providers.

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

item.title: Critical infrastructure giant Itron says it was hacked
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The American technology giant provides water and energy monitoring and utility meters to hundreds of millions of homes and businesses.

item.title: Spotify’s next frontier: Fitness content
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Spotify is adding fitness as its next major category, launching workout videos, playlists, and Peloton classes inside the app for free and Premium users.
